NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. On stringed instruments. A Psalm of Asaph. A Song. In Judah God is known; His name is great in Israel.
NKJV.2: In Salem also is His tabernacle, And His dwelling place in Zion.
NKJV.3: There He broke the arrows of the bow, The shield and sword of battle. Selah
NKJV.4: You are more glorious and excellent Than the mountains of prey.
NKJV.5: The stouthearted were plundered;  They have sunk into their sleep; And none of the mighty men have found the use of their hands.
NKJV.6: At Your rebuke, O God of Jacob, Both the chariot and horse were cast into a dead sleep.
NKJV.7: You, Yourself, are to be feared; And who may stand in Your presence When once You are angry?
NKJV.8: You caused judgment to be heard from heaven;  The earth feared and was still,
NKJV.9: When God arose to judgment, To deliver all the oppressed of the earth. Selah
NKJV.10: Surely the wrath of man shall praise You; With the remainder of wrath You shall gird Yourself.
NKJV.11: Make vows to the LORD your God, and pay them; Let all who are around Him bring presents to Him who ought to be feared.
NKJV.12: He shall cut off the spirit of princes; He is awesome to the kings of the earth.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> on Neginoth<S>5058</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> or Song<S>7892</S> of Asaph<S>623</S>. In Judah<S>3063</S> is God<S>430</S> known<S>3045</S>: his name<S>8034</S> is great<S>1419</S> in Israel<S>3478</S>. <sup>of: or, for</sup>
KJV.2: In Salem<S>8004</S> also is his tabernacle<S>5520</S>, and his dwelling place<S>4585</S> in Zion<S>6726</S>.
KJV.3: There brake<S>7665</S> he the arrows<S>7565</S> of the bow<S>7198</S>, the shield<S>4043</S>, and the sword<S>2719</S>, and the battle<S>4421</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.4: Thou art more glorious<S>215</S> and excellent<S>117</S> than the mountains<S>2042</S> of prey<S>2964</S>.
KJV.5: The stouthearted<S>47</S> <S>3820</S> are spoiled<S>7997</S>, they have slept<S>5123</S> their sleep<S>8142</S>: and none of the men<S>582</S> of might<S>2428</S> have found<S>4672</S> their hands<S>3027</S>.
KJV.6: At thy rebuke<S>1606</S>, O God<S>430</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S>, both the chariot<S>7393</S> and horse<S>5483</S> are cast into a dead sleep<S>7290</S>.
KJV.7: Thou, even thou, art to be feared<S>3372</S>: and who may stand<S>5975</S> in thy sight<S>6440</S> when<S>227</S> once thou art angry<S>639</S>?
KJV.8: Thou didst cause judgment<S>1779</S> to be heard<S>8085</S> from heaven<S>8064</S>; the earth<S>776</S> feared<S>3372</S>, and was still<S>8252</S>,
KJV.9: When God<S>430</S> arose<S>6965</S> to judgment<S>4941</S>, to save<S>3467</S> all the meek<S>6035</S> of the earth<S>776</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.10: Surely the wrath<S>2534</S> of man<S>120</S> shall praise<S>3034</S> thee: the remainder<S>7611</S> of wrath<S>2534</S> shalt thou restrain<S>2296</S>.
KJV.11: Vow<S>5087</S>, and pay<S>7999</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S> your God<S>430</S>: let all that be round about<S>5439</S> him bring<S>2986</S> presents<S>7862</S> unto him that ought to be feared<S>4172</S>. <sup>unto him: Heb. to fear</sup>
KJV.12: He shall cut off<S>1219</S> the spirit<S>7307</S> of princes<S>5057</S>: he is terrible<S>3372</S> to the kings<S>4428</S> of the earth<S>776</S>.

ESV.1: In Judah God is known; his name is great in Israel.
ESV.2: His abode has been established in Salem, his dwelling place in Zion.
ESV.3: There he broke the flashing arrows, the shield, the sword, and the weapons of war. Selah
ESV.4: Glorious are you, more majestic than the mountains full of prey.
ESV.5: The stouthearted were stripped of their spoil; they sank into sleep; all the men of war were unable to use their hands.
ESV.6: At your rebuke, O God of Jacob, both rider and horse lay stunned.
ESV.7: But you, you are to be feared! Who can stand before you when once your anger is roused?
ESV.8: From the heavens you uttered judgment; the earth feared and was still,
ESV.9: when God arose to establish judgment, to save all the humble of the earth. Selah
ESV.10: Surely the wrath of man shall praise you; the remnant  of wrath you will put on like a belt.
ESV.11: Make your vows to the LORD your God and perform them; let all around him bring gifts to him who is to be feared,
ESV.12: who cuts off the spirit of princes, who is to be feared by the kings of the earth.

NASB.1: For the choir director; on stringed instruments. A Psalm of Asaph, a Song. God is known in Judah; His name is great in Israel.
NASB.2: His tabernacle is in Salem; His dwelling place also is in Zion.
NASB.3: There He broke the flaming arrows, The shield and the sword and the weapons of war. Selah.
NASB.4: You are resplendent, More majestic than the mountains of prey.
NASB.5: The stouthearted were plundered, They sank into sleep; And none of the warriors could use his hands.
NASB.6: At Your rebuke, O God of Jacob, Both rider and horse were cast into a dead sleep.
NASB.7: You, even You, are to be feared; And who may stand in Your presence when once You are angry?
NASB.8: You caused judgment to be heard from heaven; The earth feared and was still
NASB.9: When God arose to judgment, To save all the humble of the earth. Selah.
NASB.10: For the wrath of man shall praise You; With a remnant of wrath You will gird Yourself.
NASB.11: Make vows to the LORD your God and fulfill [them]; Let all who are around Him bring gifts to Him who is to be feared.
NASB.12: He will cut off the spirit of princes; He is feared by the kings of the earth.

NLT.1:
God is honored in Judah;
his name is great in Israel.
NLT.2:
Jerusalem is where he lives;
Mount Zion is his home.
NLT.3:
There he has broken the fiery arrows of the enemy,
the shields and swords and weapons of war. Interlude
NLT.4:
You are glorious and more majestic
than the everlasting mountains.
NLT.5:
Our boldest enemies have been plundered.
They lie before us in the sleep of death.
No warrior could lift a hand against us.
NLT.6:
At the blast of your breath, O God of Jacob,
their horses and chariots lay still.
NLT.7:
No wonder you are greatly feared!
Who can stand before you when your anger explodes?
NLT.8:
From heaven you sentenced your enemies;
the earth trembled and stood silent before you.
NLT.9:
You stand up to judge those who do evil, O God,
and to rescue the oppressed of the earth. Interlude
NLT.10:
Human defiance only enhances your glory,
for you use it as a weapon.
NLT.11:
Make vows to the LORD your God, and keep them.
Let everyone bring tribute to the Awesome One.
NLT.12:
For he breaks the pride of princes,
and the kings of the earth fear him.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. On stringed instruments. A Psalm by Asaph. A song. In Judah, God is known. His name is great in Israel.
WEB.2: His tabernacle is also in Salem; His dwelling place in Zion.
WEB.3: There he broke the flaming arrows of the bow, the shield, and the sword, and the weapons of war. Selah.
WEB.4: Glorious are you, and excellent, more than mountains of game.
WEB.5: Valiant men lie plundered, they have slept their last sleep. None of the men of war can lift their hands.
WEB.6: At your rebuke, God of Jacob, both chariot and horse are cast into a dead sleep.
WEB.7: You, even you, are to be feared. Who can stand in your sight when you are angry?
WEB.8: You pronounced judgment from heaven. The earth feared, and was silent,
WEB.9: when God arose to judgment, to save all the afflicted ones of the earth. Selah.
WEB.10: Surely the wrath of man praises you. The survivors of your wrath are restrained.
WEB.11: Make vows to Yahweh your God, and fulfill them! Let all of his neighbors bring presents to him who is to be feared.
WEB.12: He will cut off the spirit of princes. He is feared by the kings of the earth.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: with stringed instruments. A psalm of Asaph. ⓚ A song. [1] God is known in Judah;
his name is great in Israel. ⓛ
CSB17.2:
His tent is in Salem,
his dwelling place in Zion. ⓜ
CSB17.3:
There he shatters the bow’s flaming arrows,
the shield, the sword, and the weapons of war. ⓝ Selah
CSB17.4:
You are resplendent and majestic
coming down from the mountains of prey. ⓐ
CSB17.5:
The brave-hearted have been plundered;
they have slipped into their final sleep.
None of the warriors was able to lift a hand. ⓑ
CSB17.6:
At your rebuke, God of Jacob,
both chariot and horse lay still. ⓒ
CSB17.7:
And you — you are to be feared. [1]
When you are angry,
who can stand before you? ⓓ
CSB17.8:
From heaven you pronounced judgment.
The earth feared and grew quiet ⓔ
CSB17.9:
when God rose up to judge
and to save all the lowly of the earth. ⓕ Selah
CSB17.10:
Even human wrath will praise you;
you will clothe yourself
with the wrath that remains. [2] ⓖ
CSB17.11:
Make and keep your vows
to the LORD your God; ⓗ
let all who are around him bring tribute
to the awe-inspiring one. [3] ⓘ
CSB17.12:
He humbles the spirit of leaders;
he is feared by the kings of the earth. ⓙ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; on stringed instruments. A Psalm of Asaph. A Song. God is known in Judah;
His name is great in Israel.
AMP.2:
His tabernacle is in Salem (Jerusalem);
His dwelling place is in Zion.
AMP.3:
There He broke the flaming arrows,
The shield, the sword, and the weapons of war. Selah.
AMP.4:
You are glorious and resplendent,
More majestic than the mountains of prey.
AMP.5:
The stouthearted have been stripped of their spoil,
They have slept the sleep [of death];
And none of the warriors could use his hands.
AMP.6:
At Your rebuke, O God of Jacob,
Both rider and horse were cast into a dead sleep [of death].
AMP.7:
You, even You, are to be feared [with the submissive wonder of reverence];
Who may stand in Your presence when once You are angry?
AMP.8:
You caused judgment to be heard from heaven;
The earth feared and was quiet
AMP.9:
When God arose to [establish] judgment,
To save all the humble of the earth. Selah.
AMP.10:
For the wrath of man shall praise You;
With a remnant of wrath You will clothe and arm Yourself.
AMP.11:
Make vows to the LORD your God and fulfill them;
Let all who are around Him bring gifts to Him who is to be feared [with awe-inspired reverence].
AMP.12:
He will cut off the spirit of princes;
He is awesome and feared by the kings of the earth.

MSG.1:
An Asaph psalm
God is well-known in Judah;
in Israel, he's a household name.
MSG.2:
He keeps a house in Salem,
his own suite of rooms in Zion.
MSG.3:
That's where, using arrows for kindling,
he made a bonfire of weapons of war.
MSG.4:
Oh, how bright you shine!
Outshining their huge piles of loot!
MSG.5:
The warriors were plundered
and left there impotent.
And now there's nothing to them,
nothing to show for their swagger and threats.
MSG.6:
Your sudden roar, God of Jacob,
knocked the wind out of horse and rider.
MSG.7:
Fierce you are, and fearsome!
Who can stand up to your rising anger?
MSG.8:
From heaven you thunder judgment;
earth falls to her knees and holds her breath.
MSG.9:
God stands tall and makes things right,
he saves all the wretched on earth.
MSG.10:
Instead of smoldering rage—God-praise!
All that sputtering rage—now a garland for God!
MSG.11:
Do for God what you said you'd do—
he is, after all, your God.
Let everyone in town bring offerings
to the One Who Watches our every move.
MSG.12:
Nobody gets by with anything,
no one plays fast and loose with him.

NIV.1:
Psalm 76
For the director of music. With stringed instruments. A psalm of Asaph. A song.
In Judah God is known;
his name is great in Israel.
NIV.2:
His tent is in Salem,
his dwelling place in Zion.
NIV.3:
There he broke the flashing arrows,
the shields and the swords, the weapons of war. Selah
NIV.4:
You are resplendent with light,
more majestic than mountains rich with game.
NIV.5:
Valiant men lie plundered,
they sleep their last sleep;
not one of the warriors
can lift his hands.
NIV.6:
At your rebuke, O God of Jacob,
both horse and chariot lie still.
NIV.7:
You alone are to be feared.
Who can stand before you when you are angry?
NIV.8:
From heaven you pronounced judgment,
and the land feared and was quiet—
NIV.9:
when you, O God, rose up to judge,
to save all the afflicted of the land. Selah
NIV.10:
Surely your wrath against men brings you praise,
and the survivors of your wrath are restrained.
NIV.11:
Make vows to the Lord your God and fulfill them;
let all the neighboring lands
bring gifts to the One to be feared.
NIV.12:
He breaks the spirit of rulers;
he is feared by the kings of the earth.

NRSVCE.1:
In Judah God is known,
his name is great in Israel.
NRSVCE.2:
His abode has been established in Salem,
his dwelling place in Zion.
NRSVCE.3:
There he broke the flashing arrows,
the shield, the sword, and the weapons of war. Selah
NRSVCE.4:
Glorious are you, more majestic
than the everlasting mountains.
NRSVCE.5:
The stouthearted were stripped of their spoil;
they sank into sleep;
none of the troops
was able to lift a hand.
NRSVCE.6:
At your rebuke, O God of Jacob,
both rider and horse lay stunned.
NRSVCE.7:
But you indeed are awesome!
Who can stand before you
when once your anger is roused?
NRSVCE.8:
From the heavens you uttered judgment;
the earth feared and was still
NRSVCE.9:
when God rose up to establish judgment,
to save all the oppressed of the earth. Selah
NRSVCE.10:
Human wrath serves only to praise you,
when you bind the last bit of your wrath around you.
NRSVCE.11:
Make vows to the LORD your God, and perform them;
let all who are around him bring gifts
to the one who is awesome,
NRSVCE.12:
who cuts off the spirit of princes,
who inspires fear in the kings of the earth.

NET.1: God has revealed himself in Judah; in Israel his reputation is great.
NET.2: He lives in Salem; he dwells in Zion.
NET.3: There he shattered the arrows, the shield, the sword, and the rest of the weapons of war. (Selah)
NET.4: You shine brightly and reveal your majesty, as you descend from the hills where you killed your prey.
NET.5: The bravehearted were plundered; they “fell asleep.” All the warriors were helpless.
NET.6: At the sound of your battle cry, O God of Jacob, both rider and horse “fell asleep.”
NET.7: You are awesome! Yes, you! Who can withstand your intense anger?
NET.8: From heaven you announced what their punishment would be. The earth was afraid and silent
NET.9: when God arose to execute judgment, and to deliver all the oppressed of the earth. (Selah)
NET.10: Certainly your angry judgment upon men will bring you praise; you reveal your anger in full measure.
NET.11: Make vows to the LORD your God and repay them! Let all those who surround him bring tribute to the awesome one!
NET.12: He humbles princes; the kings of the earth regard him as awesome.
